title: Pippa and the Winged Dreams
cover_prompt: A small, round creature with soft feathered wings, Pippa, peeking through a moonlit window with glowing dreams fluttering like moths around her. The scene is painted in warm watercolors, with deep blues and gentle golds, and a cozy bedroom softly lit by a nightlight.
lyrics:
The dreams are out tonight
Softly tapping on the pane
Wings like whispers, light as rain
Looking for a sleepy sight

One just brushed your window sill
Dancing on a silver thread
Almost tucked you into bed
But you stirred and made it still

[chorus]
Close your eyes and they will come
Dreams will find their way to you
Tuck you in with skies of blue
Better close them, little one

One dream wore a coat of gold
Tried to tell you stories sweet
But you wiggled tiny feet
Now it waits out in the cold

[chorus]
Close your eyes and they will come
Dreams will find their way to you
Tuck you in with skies of blue
Better close them, little one

Better close your eyes
style_prompt: Playful mysterious lullaby, warm female vocal, soft woodwind and low flute, 64 BPM, mischievous, conspiratorial, whimsical, fairy tale energy, like sharing a secret, mysterious but gentle, storytelling melody, soft woodwind accompaniment

character.name: Pippa
character.identity: A tiny, gentle dream-catcher who watches over children as they sleep.
character.special: She knows every dream by name and helps them find the right little one to visit.

about: A playful, conspiratorial lullaby where dreams flutter just out of reach, waiting for little ones to close their eyes. The gentle fear of missing out (FOMO) on magical dreams encourages children to settle in, while Pippa’s warm presence reassures them that sleep will bring the best adventures.
